---
title: "Features"
sidebar: en
---

Atelier combines two complete WebAssembly runtimes under a single monospaced interface. Every module below works strictly on-device.

![](../_screens/android-main.png){width=320}

## Modules

| Tab | What it does |
|-----|--------------|
| **Editor**   | Monospaced code editor with syntax highlighting; runs into the active kernel. |
| **Console**  | Interactive REPL for the active kernel (R or Python). |
| **Files**    | Browser for the workspace folder; read, write, rename, share. |
| **Plots**    | Gallery of generated plots; each exportable as PNG. |
| **Packages** | Per-kernel package management (R: `webr::install`, Python: `micropip`). |
| **Settings** | Theme (Light/Dark/System), font size, workspace path, kernel reset. |

## Kernels and libraries

| Kernel | Runtime | Bundled libraries |
|--------|---------|-------------------|
| **R**      | WebR (R 4.6 in WebAssembly) | base, stats, graphics, methods, utils; more via `webr::install`. |
| **Python** | Pyodide                     | `numpy`, `pandas`, `scipy`, `matplotlib`, `scikit-learn`. |

Both kernels share the same `/workspace` path: a file written in R is readable from Python without conversion.

## Plots

Graphics from base R (`plot(...)`), ggplot2 (`ggplot(...) + ...`) and matplotlib (`plt.savefig(...)`) are collected into the plot gallery, and each can be shared or saved individually as PNG.

## Offline guarantee

There is no built-in network access. The only point where the app would fetch bytes over the network is when you explicitly install a package via `webr::install` or `micropip` from the console — a deliberate user action.

## Where to get it

| Channel | Status |
|---------|--------|
| Android (APK) | **Development build** — the only current channel, via [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/etabli-dev/etabli-atelier/releases) |
| App Store (iOS) · Google Play (Android) | planned — not yet available |
| F-Droid — Établi's own repository | planned — not yet available (will ship pre-built WASM runtimes, so it is not intended for the main F-Droid repo) |
| Source code | [`etabli-dev/etabli-atelier`](https://github.com/etabli-dev/etabli-atelier) |
